title: Exodus: Tabernacle Presence
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of exodus: tabernacle presence and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study exodus: tabernacle presence and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: What was the primary theological purpose of the tabernacle God commanded Israel to build in Exodus 25-31? Answer: to be a dwelling place where God's presence could dwell among his people.
segments.correct_answer: for God to dwell among his people — a sanctuary for his presence
acceptable_variants: so God could live in the midst of Israel
a portable sanctuary for God's presence among Israel
a dwelling place for Yahweh to tabernacle with Israel

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The portable sanctuary God commanded Israel to build so his glory could dwell among them; its design reflected Eden and anticipated the temple and ultimately Christ
The innermost room of the tabernacle, separated by a veil, containing the ark of the covenant; only the high priest could enter it once a year on the Day of Atonement
